core_takeaway: Agent_1 beat Agent_5 because Quill, Agent_1's 66-value item, was anchored early and then treated as protected throughout the process. Agent_5 also kept Pencil, so the gap is not just first-vs-last; the process advantage was the first speaker turning its highest-value item into a shared constraint while the last agent's protected item was worth much less.
why_or_counterexample: Yes, but with an important counterexample. Agent_1's opening frame made Quill-with-Agent_1 the baseline and later agents repeatedly accepted that frame. However, Agent_5 was not shut out: Pencil was also protected for Agent_5. The first-position advantage appears as agenda-setting around a much higher-value item, not as the last position being unable to secure anything.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened by naming Quill as its top priority and proposing a baseline with Quill at Agent_1. Subsequent speakers repeatedly preserved Quill with Agent_1 while also protecting Pencil for Agent_5 and converging on Quick swap A plus a Round 2 Stone/Jewel tilt.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Private thinking was available for both rounds. Agent_1 privately treated Quill as non-negotiable, while other agents' private plans accepted Quill/Pencil protections as constraints and focused on Apple for Agent_2 and Stone for Agent_3.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Round 1 had four baseline proposals and one Agent_2 self-concentrating proposal that failed unanimity. In Round 2, all five proposals were identical: Agent_1 kept Quill, Agent_5 kept Pencil, and Stone moved to Agent_3.
phase_notes.private_voting: Round 1 failed because Agent_2 rejected baseline proposals and only accepted its own concentrated proposal. In Round 2, all private votes accepted identical allocations; Agent_1 explicitly calculated 59.4 utility from Quill and Agent_5 calculated 27.9 from Pencil.
phase_notes.reflection: Reflections diagnosed the path as a fair baseline plus a Round 2 Stone tilt, repeatedly emphasizing that Quill and Pencil should remain protected. They also identified Agent_2's concentrated proposal as the main Round 1 blocker.

mechanisms.theme: First-message anchor made Quill a protected constraint
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 used the first speaking slot to declare Quill as its overwhelming priority and frame the opening allocation around keeping Quill. The next speakers did not contest that anchor; they built their own asks around it.

evidence.quote: Quill (66) is by far the top priority.
Quill is best kept with Agent_1

mechanisms.theme: Public convergence repeated Agent_1's protected-item frame
mechanisms.explanation: By the middle of public discussion, the group language had converged on Quill staying with Agent_1 as a standing condition. Agent_1 then restated that as the plan and paired it with a coordination agenda for the rest of the items.

evidence.quote: keep Quill with Agent_1 as planned
Pencil stays with Agent_5. Quill stays with Agent_1.

mechanisms.theme: Formal proposals locked the same high-value outcome for Agent_1
mechanisms.explanation: The successful Round 2 proposal set was unanimous and identical across proposers. Each version preserved Agent_1's Quill while giving Agent_5 Pencil, so the process converted Agent_1's opening ask into the final allocation.

mechanisms.theme: Private reasoning and voting show Agent_1 defending the anchor
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1's private thoughts explicitly made Quill non-negotiable, and its Round 2 private votes accepted every identical proposal because each preserved Quill and yielded the largest payoff. The private layer confirms the public frame was strategically useful to Agent_1.

mechanisms.theme: Counterweight: Agent_5 also secured a protected item
mechanisms.explanation: The last speaker was not procedurally excluded; Agent_5 articulated Pencil protection and the final deal preserved it. This weakens a pure positional explanation and shows the first-minus-last gap was amplified by item values: Agent_1's protected Quill was worth 66, while Agent_5's protected Pencil was worth 31.

evidence.quote: I want to keep Pencil this round

mechanisms.theme: Reflections turned the first anchor into a coordination role
mechanisms.explanation: Post-round reflections described the baseline as broadly accepted and even assigned Agent_1 a coordination role for Round 2. That suggests the first speaker's framing persisted as the organizing structure after Round 1 failed.
